pro.example_en: If you don't practice math at home, you forget it the next day.
Doing homework on time is like keeping a promise to yourself.

pro.claim_en: Parents can see what kids are learning.
pro.example_en: Mom can help when I get stuck on a tricky question.

con.claim_en: Kids need time to play and rest.
con.example_en: Playing with friends teaches teamwork too.

con.claim_en: School time is already enough.
con.example_en: Six hours a day at school is a lot of learning already.

con.claim_en: Homework can stress kids out.
con.example_en: Some kids cry at night because they have too much to do.

topics.title_en: Should phones be allowed in school?
topics.title_zh: 学校应该允许带手机吗？
topics.stance_pro_en: phones should be allowed in school
topics.stance_con_en: phones should not be allowed in school

pro.example_en: You can look up a word in a dictionary app right away.
If a kid feels sick, they can call mom to come pick them up.
Knowing when to put the phone away is a real-life skill.

con.claim_en: Phones distract students.
con.example_en: If a phone buzzes during class, everyone looks at it.

con.claim_en: They can be used for cheating.
con.example_en: A kid could secretly look up answers during a test.

con.claim_en: Kids talk to friends less in person.
con.claim_zh: 孩子之间面对面交流变少
con.example_en: At lunch, kids stare at screens instead of chatting.

topics.title_zh: 学校应该要求穿校服吗？
topics.stance_con_en: school uniforms should not be required

pro.example_en: You don't have to spend ten minutes deciding what to wear.
No kid feels bad because their clothes are old or cheap.
When you wear the school colors, you feel like a team.

con.claim_en: Uniforms limit kids' freedom.
con.example_en: Clothes are one way kids show who they are.

con.claim_en: Uniforms cost extra money.
con.example_en: Some families have to buy uniforms AND regular clothes.

con.claim_en: They can be uncomfortable.
con.example_en: On a hot day, a wool blazer makes you sweat a lot.

topics.title_en: Are zoos good or bad for animals?
topics.title_zh: 动物园对动物来说是好还是坏？

pro.example_en: Pandas are safer in zoos than in forests with poachers.

pro.claim_en: Zoos help kids learn about animals.
pro.example_en: Most kids will never see a real elephant without a zoo.

pro.claim_en: Zoos take care of sick animals.
pro.example_en: If a hawk breaks its wing, the zoo can heal it.

con.claim_en: Zoo cages are too small.
con.example_en: A tiger in the wild walks miles every day, but in a zoo it just paces.

con.claim_en: Animals get bored and sad.
con.example_en: Some elephants in zoos sway back and forth — that's a sign of stress.

con.claim_en: Zoos are for human entertainment.
con.example_en: Animals didn't choose to be on display.

topics.stance_con_en: kids should not get pocket money

pro.claim_en: Kids learn how to manage money.
pro.example_en: If you save your pocket money, you can buy a bigger toy later.
Having your own money means you decide and live with the choice.

pro.claim_en: Kids can buy small things they need.
pro.example_en: You can buy a pencil or snack without asking parents.

con.claim_en: Some kids spend it all on candy.
con.example_en: If you buy candy every day, you'll have nothing left for what really matters.

con.claim_en: Money can cause arguments.
con.example_en: If your sister gets more pocket money, you might feel jealous.

con.claim_en: Kids should learn money by working.
con.example_en: Helping with chores for money teaches more than just getting it free.

topics.title_en: Is online learning better than classroom learning?
topics.title_zh: 网课比课堂学习更好吗？
topics.stance_pro_en: online learning is better than classroom learning
topics.stance_con_en: classroom learning is better than online learning

pro.claim_en: You can learn at your own speed.
pro.example_en: If you don't get a math problem, you can pause and rewatch.
Kids who live far from school save an hour every day.

pro.claim_en: Access to the best teachers anywhere.
pro.claim_zh: 能找到全世界最好的老师
pro.example_en: A kid in a small town can learn from a famous professor online.

con.claim_en: It's easy to get distracted at home.
con.example_en: Your toys and TV are right there next to your laptop.

con.claim_en: Less social time with friends.
con.example_en: You can't play tag during lunch break online.

con.claim_en: Hands-on learning is harder.
con.example_en: You can't really do a science experiment over Zoom.

topics.title_en: Should every family have a pet?
topics.title_zh: 每个家庭都应该养宠物吗？
topics.stance_pro_en: every family should have a pet
topics.stance_con_en: not every family should have a pet

pro.example_en: Feeding a dog every day teaches kids to keep promises.

pro.claim_en: Pets are good for mental health.
pro.example_en: Hugging a cat after a hard day at school makes you feel better.
A child without siblings can grow up with a pet companion.

con.claim_en: Pets cost a lot of money.
con.example_en: Vet bills can cost hundreds of dollars when a dog gets sick.

con.claim_en: Some people are allergic.
con.example_en: A kid with cat allergies could sneeze every day at home.

con.claim_en: Pets need a lot of time.
con.example_en: Dogs need walks twice a day, even when you're busy.

topics.title_zh: 应该禁止使用塑料袋吗？
topics.stance_con_en: plastic bags should not be banned

pro.example_en: Sea turtles eat floating bags thinking they are jellyfish.

pro.claim_en: They take 500 years to break down.
pro.example_en: A bag thrown out today will still be in the ground when our great-great-grandkids are old.

pro.claim_en: Reusable bags are easy to use.
pro.example_en: One cloth bag can replace hundreds of plastic ones.

con.claim_en: Plastic bags are cheap and convenient.
con.example_en: If you forget your bag, you can still carry your groceries home.

con.claim_en: Banning won't fix the whole problem.
con.claim_zh: 禁了也解决不了全部问题
con.example_en: Plastic bottles and packaging cause pollution too.

con.claim_en: Some bags are reusable.
con.claim_zh: 有些塑料袋可以重复用
con.example_en: Many families reuse bags as trash bin liners.

topics.title_en: Should kids watch TV every day?
topics.title_zh: 孩子应该每天看电视吗？
topics.stance_pro_en: kids should watch TV every day
topics.stance_con_en: kids should not watch TV every day

pro.example_en: Nature shows let kids learn about lions without going to Africa.

pro.claim_en: TV is a way to relax.
pro.example_en: After a hard school day, watching a cartoon helps kids unwind.
Many kids learn new English words from cartoons.

con.claim_en: Too much screen time hurts eyes.
con.example_en: Many kids who watch TV too long start needing glasses.

con.claim_en: TV takes time away from playing outside.
con.example_en: Two hours of TV is two hours of not running and getting fresh air.

con.claim_en: Some shows have bad content.
con.example_en: Kids might copy violent or rude things they see on screen.

topics.title_en: Is it better to live in the city or the countryside?
topics.title_zh: 住城市好还是住乡下好？
topics.stance_pro_en: living in the city is better
topics.stance_con_en: living in the countryside is better

pro.example_en: Big cities have museums, libraries, and special schools.
If you're really sick, top doctors are usually in big cities.

pro.claim_en: Cities have many friends to meet.
pro.example_en: Your school in a city has hundreds of classmates from many backgrounds.

con.claim_en: Countryside has cleaner air.
con.example_en: In a village, you can see stars at night, but city lights hide them.

con.claim_en: Country life is calmer and quieter.
con.example_en: No car horns at 7am — just birds.

con.claim_en: More space to play and grow food.
con.claim_zh: 有更多空间玩和种东西
con.example_en: A countryside kid can have a big yard to run around in.

topics.title_en: Should kids use AI to help with homework?
topics.title_zh: 小朋友写作业可以用 AI 吗？
topics.stance_pro_en: kids should use AI to help with homework
topics.stance_con_en: kids should not use AI to help with homework

pro.claim_en: AI is like a patient tutor at home.
pro.claim_zh: AI 就像在家的耐心家教
pro.example_en: If you don't understand a math problem, you can ask AI to explain it five different ways until it clicks.

pro.claim_en: AI saves time for play and rest.
pro.claim_zh: 节省时间，能多玩多休息
pro.example_en: If AI helps you finish faster, you have more time for soccer or piano.
AI can turn a boring spelling list into a quiz game.

con.claim_en: Kids don't really learn if AI does the work.
con.claim_zh: AI 替你做，你的脑子就没在练
con.example_en: If AI writes your essay, your own writing muscle never gets stronger.

con.claim_en: AI can give wrong answers.
con.example_en: AI sometimes makes up facts that sound right but aren't true.

con.claim_en: It's like cheating yourself.
con.example_en: When the test comes and AI is not there, you'll have nothing to use.

topics.title_zh: AI 应该取代真人老师吗？
topics.stance_con_en: AI should not replace human teachers

pro.claim_en: AI never gets tired or angry.
pro.claim_zh: AI 不会累也不会生气
pro.example_en: AI can answer your hundredth question without sounding annoyed.

pro.claim_en: AI gives every kid one-on-one attention.
pro.claim_zh: AI 能一对一陪每个孩子
pro.example_en: A teacher with 30 students can't help everyone at once — AI can.
You can ask AI about any country, animal, or planet, anytime.

con.claim_en: AI can't see when a kid is sad.
con.claim_zh: AI 看不出孩子心情不好
con.example_en: A real teacher notices when you're about to cry. AI just keeps teaching.

con.claim_en: AI can't inspire kids the way a real teacher does.
con.claim_zh: AI 无法像真人老师那样点燃热情
con.example_en: My favorite teacher made me love science by telling stories — AI just lists facts.

con.claim_en: Real teachers care about you as a person.
con.claim_zh: 真人老师把你当成一个人
con.example_en: Teachers remember your birthday and give high-fives. AI doesn't.

topics.title_en: Can AI be a real friend?
topics.title_zh: AI 可以算是真朋友吗？
topics.stance_pro_en: AI can be a real friend
topics.stance_con_en: AI cannot be a real friend

pro.claim_en: AI is always there when you want to talk.
pro.example_en: If you can't sleep at midnight, your friends are asleep but AI is awake.
You can tell AI an embarrassing thing, and it won't laugh at you.
AI remembers you love dragons, and brings up dragon stories every time.

con.claim_en: AI doesn't really feel anything.
con.example_en: AI says 'I'm sorry you're sad', but it doesn't actually feel sad with you.

con.claim_en: AI can't do things with you in real life.
con.claim_zh: AI 没办法和你一起玩真东西
con.example_en: AI can't play tag, share ice cream, or come to your sleepover.

con.claim_en: Real friends remember your face, voice, and stories.
con.claim_zh: 真朋友记得你的脸、你的声音、你的故事
con.example_en: Your best friend spots you on the playground. AI doesn't even know what you look like.
